project: Influx v1 — PRD 10: OTEL Telemetry + Rejection-Rate Logging + Final Polish
description: Final v1 PRD covering S-14 (OTEL telemetry) and S-15 (rejection-rate logging + stub removal) at Milestone M4. Adds an opt-in, additive, optional-dependency OTEL telemetry wrapper at `src/influx/telemetry.py` that is a no-op when OTEL packages are absent and when `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED` is not set. When enabled, it emits the FR-OBS-4 spans (`influx.run`, `influx.fetch.arxiv`, `influx.fetch.rss`, `influx.filter`, `influx.enrich.tier1`, `influx.enrich.tier2`, `influx.enrich.tier3`, `influx.lithos.write`, `influx.lithos.retrieve`, `influx.archive.download`) carrying the `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.run_type`, `influx.source`, `influx.item_count` attributes, with optional console fallback (`INFLUX_OTEL_CONSOLE_FALLBACK=true`) when no collector is configured. Adds per-tag rejection-rate logging every `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs` runs per profile, backed by an in-memory counter (restart resets — FR-OBS-5 is informative only). Final polish strand removes any remaining `TODO(PRD-NN)` / `# STUB:` / temporary-seam markers introduced in PRDs 01-09, asserts AC-X-1 (no hardcoded constants outside config parsing), AC-X-2 (provider swap works without code change via `[models.extract]` `provider` switch from `anthropic` to `openrouter`), and AC-X-6 (≥80% coverage on pure modules; happy-path + error-envelope contract tests for every Lithos tool called by Influx). Updates Docker artefacts per master PRD §9.4.4 if not already done. After this PRD, v1 is functionally complete and a v1 tag candidate.

userStories.title: Add OTEL optional dependency group and no-op telemetry wrapper module
userStories.description: As an Influx operator, I want a telemetry wrapper module that is a no-op when OTEL is disabled or its optional packages are absent so that the base install runs identically with or without OTEL (FR-OBS-2, AC-M4-3, AC-10-A).
acceptanceCriteria: Add `[project.optional-dependencies] otel = ["opentelemetry-api", "opentelemetry-sdk", "opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-http"]` to `pyproject.toml`
Create `src/influx/telemetry.py` exposing a thin wrapper for span creation and attribute setting whose default behaviour is a no-op (concrete helper API is left to the implementer)

OTEL enablement is env-driven via `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED` with default false (FR-OBS-2, PRD §2.1 / §5.2). The wrapper is a no-op whenever OTEL is not enabled — including the default case where the env var is unset — performing no span creation and no attribute-setting calls (the body is a dictionary lookup / attribute access, not object instantiation — AC-10-A)

AC-M4-3 (service-level): with OTEL optional packages absent and OTEL disabled, the service starts, serves requests, and completes a run. The acceptance test exercises this at the service level (`influx serve` boot + a representative run). Import-stubbing of the OTEL packages is permitted as the test mechanism for simulating absent extras when a separate virtualenv path is impractical in CI; the assertion remains the service-level behaviour (start + serve + run), not just module import or wrapper no-op

Unit test `tests/unit/test_telemetry.py` asserts: (1) OTEL is off by default — the wrapper is a no-op when `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED` is not set (FR-OBS-2); (2) the wrapper is a no-op when `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=false` (FR-OBS-2); (3) the wrapper is enabled when `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=true` and OTEL optional packages are installed; (4) the wrapper remains a no-op when OTEL optional packages are simulated absent via import-stub regardless of `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED` (AC-M4-3)
Wrapper does not raise in any of the no-op paths (when invoked with documented/valid wrapper calls while OTEL is disabled or its optional packages are absent)

Packaging smoke check: `uv pip install .[otel]` (or an equivalent extras-resolution check such as `pip install .[otel]` / `uv pip compile --extra otel`) succeeds against the updated `pyproject.toml` so the optional-extras group resolves cleanly (Definition of Done item)
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass
userStories.notes: Implementation guidance (non-binding): AC-M4-3 is a service-level contract (start + serve + complete a run with OTEL extras absent and OTEL disabled). Per PRD §7.4 / AC-M4-3, the preferred verification path is a virtualenv without the `otel` extras; if that is impractical in CI, simulate the missing extras via import-stubbing while still asserting the service-level behaviour rather than just module import. This is independent of the Docker story (US-010), which is scoped to `/archive`, port 8080, and the `CMD` line.

userStories.title: Telemetry wrapper enabled path: span creation, attributes, and console fallback exporter
userStories.description: As an Influx operator, I want the telemetry wrapper to emit OTEL spans when enabled and optionally print spans to stdout when no collector is configured so that I can observe runs locally without infrastructure (FR-OBS-2, FR-OBS-3, AC-M4-1).
acceptanceCriteria: When `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=true` and OTEL optional packages are installed, the wrapper creates real OTEL spans for the names passed to it
Attributes passed to the wrapper are set on the underlying span using OTEL's attribute API
When `INFLUX_OTEL_CONSOLE_FALLBACK=true` and no OTLP collector endpoint is configured, spans are observably emitted to stdout (FR-OBS-3)

Extend `tests/unit/test_telemetry.py` with: (1) enabled wrapper creates a span with the given name; (2) enabled wrapper sets the given attributes on the span; (3) with console fallback enabled and no collector configured, spans are observable on stdout. End-to-end export to a real OTLP collector is out of scope for this unit test
When `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, no span is created even if OTEL packages are installed (AC-10-A regression guard)
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Wire telemetry spans into the run orchestration and filter call sites for influx.run and influx.filter
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the per-profile run orchestration and the filter step to emit `influx.run` and `influx.filter` spans with the documented attributes so that a complete run is observable end-to-end (FR-OBS-4, AC-10-B).
acceptanceCriteria: An `influx.run` span is created around the per-profile run lifecycle (covering both scheduled and HTTP-triggered invocations as listed in PRD §6 / FR-OBS-4) carrying `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.run_type` attributes. The exact instrumentation site (single shared orchestration entrypoint vs. per-caller wrapping) is left to the implementer

The filter step is wrapped in an `influx.filter` span at the actual scorer or its call site (e.g. in `src/influx/filter.py` and/or `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py`) carrying `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.item_count` attributes
All wrapping uses the `src/influx/telemetry.py` wrapper from US-001/US-002 — no direct `opentelemetry.*` imports leak into the run orchestration or filter modules
With OTEL disabled, run orchestration and filter behaviour are unchanged (AC-10-A regression check via existing tests)

Unit / integration test asserts that with OTEL enabled (using the in-memory exporter pattern from `tests/unit/test_telemetry.py`), invoking the run orchestration entrypoint produces an `influx.run` span and an `influx.filter` span with the documented attribute keys
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Wire telemetry spans into source fetchers (arxiv, rss)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want each source fetcher to emit a per-fetch span so that I can attribute fetch latency and item counts to a given source within a run (FR-OBS-4).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` wraps its fetch entrypoint in an `influx.fetch.arxiv` span carrying applicable attributes from the FR-OBS-4 set (`influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.source`, `influx.item_count`)

`src/influx/sources/rss.py` wraps its fetch entrypoint in an `influx.fetch.rss` span carrying applicable attributes from the FR-OBS-4 set (`influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.source`, `influx.item_count`)
All wrapping uses the `src/influx/telemetry.py` wrapper — no direct `opentelemetry.*` imports in the source modules
With OTEL disabled, both fetchers behave identically to before (existing source tests still pass)

Unit / integration test (extending the OTEL test setup) asserts both `influx.fetch.arxiv` and `influx.fetch.rss` spans appear with the documented attribute keys when their respective fetchers run with OTEL enabled
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Wire telemetry spans for enrichment tier1/tier2/tier3 in their real callers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want each enrichment tier to emit its own span at its actual call site so that LLM-call cost / latency per tier is observable (FR-OBS-4).
acceptanceCriteria: Tier 1 is instrumented either inside `tier1_enrich()` in `src/influx/enrich.py` or at its invocation site in `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` — wrapped in an `influx.enrich.tier1` span carrying applicable attributes from the FR-OBS-4 set (`influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.item_count`)

Tier 2 is instrumented on the full-text extraction path that backs it — i.e. at `src/influx/extraction/pipeline.py` and/or its invocation site in `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` — wrapped in an `influx.enrich.tier2` span carrying applicable attributes from the FR-OBS-4 set (`influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.item_count`)

Tier 3 is instrumented either inside `tier3_extract()` in `src/influx/enrich.py` or at its invocation site in `src/influx/sources/arxiv.py` — wrapped in an `influx.enrich.tier3` span carrying applicable attributes from the FR-OBS-4 set (`influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.item_count`)
All wrapping uses the `src/influx/telemetry.py` wrapper — no direct `opentelemetry.*` imports in the enrich / extraction modules
With OTEL disabled, enrichment and extraction behaviour are unchanged (existing tests still pass)
Test asserts all three tier spans appear with the documented attribute keys when the corresponding tiers run with OTEL enabled
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Wire telemetry spans into storage / lithos_client / lcma for write, retrieve, and archive download
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Lithos writes / retrievals and archive downloads to emit spans so that storage-tier latency is observable per run (FR-OBS-4).
acceptanceCriteria: `src/influx/lithos_client.py` (or the Lithos write call sites in `src/influx/scheduler.py`) wraps Lithos writes in an `influx.lithos.write` span carrying `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id` attributes
`src/influx/lithos_client.py` (or `src/influx/lcma.py`) wraps Lithos retrievals in an `influx.lithos.retrieve` span carrying `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id` attributes

Archive download path (in `src/influx/storage.py` or wherever archive HTML is fetched / written) is wrapped in an `influx.archive.download` span carrying `influx.profile`, `influx.run_id`, `influx.source` attributes
All wrapping uses the `src/influx/telemetry.py` wrapper — no direct `opentelemetry.*` imports in storage / client modules
With OTEL disabled, storage and client modules behave identically to before (existing tests still pass)
Test asserts `influx.lithos.write`, `influx.lithos.retrieve`, and `influx.archive.download` spans all appear with the documented attribute keys when these operations run with OTEL enabled
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Integration test: in-memory OTEL exporter asserts the full FR-OBS-4 span + attribute set
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a single integration test that exercises a representative run end-to-end with OTEL enabled and asserts every FR-OBS-4 span is emitted with its documented attributes (AC-M4-1, AC-10-B).
acceptanceCriteria: Create `tests/integration/test_otel_spans.py` that configures the telemetry wrapper to emit to an in-memory OTEL `InMemorySpanExporter` (or equivalent test exporter)

One or more integration scenarios in this file collectively exercise every FR-OBS-4 span (profile + arXiv fetch + RSS fetch + filter + tier1 + tier2 + tier3 enrich + Lithos write + Lithos retrieve + archive download) with `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=true`. A single end-to-end scenario is acceptable but not required — the spans MAY be covered across multiple focused scenarios within the same file

Asserts (across the scenarios) that the union of exported spans contains the full FR-OBS-4 span list: `influx.run`, `influx.fetch.arxiv`, `influx.fetch.rss`, `influx.filter`, `influx.enrich.tier1`, `influx.enrich.tier2`, `influx.enrich.tier3`, `influx.lithos.write`, `influx.lithos.retrieve`, and `influx.archive.download` (AC-10-B, PRD §9)
For each captured span, asserts the applicable attributes from `{influx.profile, influx.run_id, influx.run_type, influx.source, influx.item_count}` are present per FR-OBS-4
A complementary assertion confirms that with `INFLUX_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, an equivalent invocation produces zero exported spans (AC-M4-2 / AC-10-A regression guard)
Test runs without requiring a live OTEL collector — only the in-memory exporter
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Per-profile rejection-rate logging cadence (every feedback.recalibrate_after_runs runs)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want per-tag rejection rates logged every `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs` runs per profile so that I can monitor filter quality drift (FR-OBS-5, AC-M4-5, AC-10-D).
acceptanceCriteria: Implement an in-memory per-profile run counter (keyed by profile name) that increments once per run for that profile (counting runs since the last recalibration log, per §2.2 of the PRD)

When the counter for a profile reaches a multiple of the global `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs` config setting, emit one structured JSON log line containing per-tag rejection rates for that profile (cadence is per profile; the threshold is read from the single root `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs` value)

Rejection rate is defined per tag, per PRD §2.2, as: the proportion of filter results carrying each tag that were subsequently rejected by the user via `influx:rejected:<profile>`. The implementation MUST retain enough information about filter-result tags to compute this metric against filter results (rather than redefining it against persisted note tags), while keeping the supporting state in-memory only and without introducing new Lithos tags (per PRD §3, out of scope: 'No new Lithos tags'). The specific mechanism for retaining filter-tag information is left to the implementer
The counter is in-memory only — a process restart resets it (FR-OBS-5 is informative only, per PRD §2.2)

Create `tests/unit/test_rejection_rate_logging.py` with a seeded state where `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs = 3`: assert the rejection-rate log line is emitted on the 3rd, 6th, and 9th runs and NOT on runs 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8 (AC-10-D)
Negative test: with `feedback.recalibrate_after_runs = 5`, the log line is NOT emitted on runs 1-4 and IS emitted on run 5
Per-profile isolation test: two profiles with independent counters emit at independent cadences
Log line is JSON-structured (matches FR-OBS-1) and includes the profile name and per-tag rejection-rate map

AC-M4-4: per-run structured logs emitted by the run orchestration include `filtered` and `ingested` counts (or equivalent named fields) per profile for that run, so operators can read those counts directly from the run's log output
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Final stub / seam removal sweep with meta-test asserting no TODO(PRD-) or STUB markers
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want every remaining `TODO(PRD-NN)` / `# STUB:` / temporary-seam marker from PRDs 01-09 removed so that PRD 10 satisfies its own §4 ('No internal seams permitted') and AC-10-C (every prior 'seams permitted' item is resolved).
acceptanceCriteria: Search the codebase for `TODO(PRD-` and `# STUB:` markers introduced in PRDs 01-09 and either remove the marker (and any code it gates) or replace with a real implementation. Broader seam cleanup (e.g. `# stub` / `# no-op` / `# temporary seam` style comments) is handled as checklist / manual-review work under AC-10-C rather than by this automated search

Add `tests/meta/test_no_stubs.py` per PRD §9 — either a dedicated search-style test or a manual review checklist embedded as that file — that asserts the codebase contains no `TODO(PRD-` or `# STUB:` markers left behind. Scope and any necessary exclusions (e.g. PRD/spec markdown, vendored trees, generated artefacts) are left to the implementer. The separate AC-10-C checklist (covered by the next criterion) handles enumeration of resolved 'Internal seams permitted' items from PRD 01-09
Meta-test passes after the sweep
An explicit PRD-01..09 'seams permitted' checklist (in this PRD's completion notes or an equivalent location) enumerates each prior-PRD seam and confirms its resolution in source (AC-10-C)
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
Full test suite passes
userStories.notes: Implementation guidance (non-binding): if the meta-test fails, listing the offending file paths and line numbers makes diagnosis easier.

userStories.title: Docker artefacts updated per master PRD §9.4.4
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `docker/Dockerfile` and `docker/docker-compose.yml` to reflect the v1 runtime contract (`/archive` mount, port 8080 exposed, `CMD ["influx", "serve"]`) so that v1 ships a runnable container (Definition of Done item).
acceptanceCriteria: `docker/Dockerfile` ends with `CMD ["influx", "serve"]` (or equivalent entrypoint that ultimately runs `influx serve`)
`docker/Dockerfile` exposes port 8080
`docker/docker-compose.yml` mounts `/archive` as a volume on the influx service
Typecheck passes
Lint (ruff) passes
All existing tests still pass

userStories.title: Final v1 gate: assert AC-X-1, AC-X-2, AC-X-6 and ≥80% pure-module coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the final v1 gate satisfied: no hardcoded constants outside config parsing (AC-X-1), provider swap works without code change (AC-X-2), pure-module coverage ≥ 80% with happy-path + error-envelope contract tests for every Lithos tool called by Influx (AC-X-6).
acceptanceCriteria: AC-X-1 (part 1 — config-driven tunability): every tunable value called out in master PRD §7.5 / the requirements (filter, enrichment, download/archive, resilience, extraction tunables) can be set in `influx.toml` and takes effect on the next process start. Evidence that tunables are config-driven and pick up new values on restart is required; the specific verification mechanism (integration test, config-roundtrip test, targeted unit/contract tests, or a documented manual smoke procedure) is left to the implementer

AC-X-1 (part 2 — no stray constants): a grep / linter check confirms no hardcoded tunable constants live in `filter.py`, `enrich.py`, `storage.py`, resilience modules, or extraction code paths — defaults live only in config-parsing code (the enforcement mechanism — ad-hoc grep, committed meta-test, or CI script — is left to the implementer)

AC-X-2 (final, per PRD §8 / master PRD §7.5): the `[models.extract]` `provider` setting is config-driven and can be switched between two supported provider blocks (e.g. swapping the configured provider for an alternate `[providers.<name>]` block) to redirect deep-extraction traffic without code changes — verified by running the integration suite with the alternate provider block. Note: the current extract caller targets an OpenAI-compatible `/chat/completions` API (see `src/influx/enrich.py`) and the shipped example config already routes `[models.extract]` through OpenRouter rather than direct Anthropic; if direct-Anthropic support is desired as one of the swappable providers, that is an explicit prerequisite outside this criterion
AC-X-6: pure-module coverage (config, URL, path, schemas, prompts, slug per master PRD §18.2) is ≥ 80% — measurement tooling and reporting mechanism are left to the implementer
AC-X-6: every Lithos tool called by Influx has both a happy-path contract test and an error-envelope contract test — enumerated explicitly in the test file or a checklist

`python -m influx validate-config` against `influx.example.toml` succeeds with the full v0.7 validation pipeline (config schema + prompt vars + JSON-mode + SSE dry-connect) — verified by an automated test or documented manual smoke step
Ruff, pyright (or equivalent typecheck), and pytest all green
Typecheck passes
